title: AI-Powered Integrated Production Optimization: Industrial Significance and Educational Impact for Process Systems Engineering
novelty_statement: This paper provides the first comprehensive analysis of how AI-driven integrated asset modelling frameworks simultaneously advance industrial production optimization on mature petroleum fields and transform engineering education through research-based teaching, using the AI-PRO project at NTNU as a concrete case study.

research_questions.question: What are the key industrial challenges in integrated production optimization on the NCS that necessitate AI-driven approaches?
research_questions.method: Analysis of current IAM limitations, computational bottlenecks, and operational gaps based on literature and industry practice
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Clear identification of at least 5 distinct industrial challenges with supporting evidence

research_questions.question: How can open-source simulation tools (NeqSim, OPM) be coupled with AI surrogates to create an operationally deployable integrated production optimization framework?
research_questions.method: Architectural analysis and design of modular digital twin coupling strategy
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Complete framework architecture with defined interfaces and data flows

research_questions.question: What is the educational value of embedding AI-driven production optimization research into engineering curricula at NTNU?
research_questions.method: Curriculum mapping, course integration analysis, and comparison with international best practices
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Concrete curriculum proposals with learning outcomes and assessment strategies

research_questions.question: How does the dual industrial-educational mission of AI-PRO create synergies that amplify impact in both domains?
research_questions.method: Analysis of feedback loops between industry case studies and student projects
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Identification of at least 3 concrete synergy mechanisms
